strings.c(3) Library Functions Manual strings.c(3)

NAME

strings.c - Session string generation and validation implementation.

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct word_cache_entry_t
Hashtable entry for cached word lookups.

Macros


#define PCRE2_CODE_UNIT_WIDTH 8

Functions


void acds_strings_destroy (void)
Cleanup function for session string cache Called by asciichat_shared_destroy() during library cleanup. Safe to call multiple times (idempotent). asciichat_error_t acds_string_init (void)
asciichat_error_t acds_string_generate (char *output, size_t output_size)
bool is_session_string (const char *str)

Detailed Description

Session string generation and validation implementation.

This module generates memorable session strings in the format: adjective-noun-noun (e.g., "swift-river-mountain")

It maintains cached hashtables of adjectives and nouns for fast O(1) validation, ensuring session strings only contain real words.

Definition in file discovery/strings.c.

Macro Definition Documentation

#define PCRE2_CODE_UNIT_WIDTH 8

Definition at line 21 of file discovery/strings.c.

Function Documentation

asciichat_error_t acds_string_generate (char * output, size_t output_size)

Definition at line 225 of file discovery/strings.c.

225                                                                          {
226   if (!output || output_size < SESSION_STRING_BUFFER_SIZE) {
227     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "output buffer must be at least %zu bytes",
228                      (size_t)SESSION_STRING_BUFFER_SIZE);
229   }
230 
231   // libsodium is guaranteed to be initialized by acds_string_init() before this is called
232   // No need to call sodium_init() again (redundant initialization removed)
233 
234   // Pick random adjective
235   uint32_t adj_idx = randombytes_uniform((uint32_t)adjectives_count);
236   const char *adj = adjectives[adj_idx];
237 
238   // Pick two random nouns
239   uint32_t noun1_idx = randombytes_uniform((uint32_t)nouns_count);
240   uint32_t noun2_idx = randombytes_uniform((uint32_t)nouns_count);
241   const char *noun1 = nouns[noun1_idx];
242   const char *noun2 = nouns[noun2_idx];
243 
244   // Format: adjective-noun-noun
245   int written = safe_snprintf(output, output_size, "%s-%s-%s", adj, noun1, noun2);
246   if (written < 0 || (size_t)written >= output_size) {
247     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_BUFFER_OVERFLOW, "Session string too long for buffer");
248   }
249 
250   log_dev("Generated session string: %s", output);
251   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
252 }




References adjectives, adjectives_count,
    nouns, nouns_count, safe_snprintf(), and
    SESSION_STRING_BUFFER_SIZE.

    options_preset_unified(), and server_main().

void
  acds_strings_destroy (void )


Cleanup function for session string cache Called by
    asciichat_shared_destroy() during library cleanup. Safe to call
    multiple times (idempotent).


Definition at line 55 of file
  discovery/strings.c.


55                                 {
56   // Shutdown if initialized
57   if (!lifecycle_shutdown(&g_cache_lc)) {
58     return; // Not initialized
59   }
60 
61   // Cleanup adjectives cache
62   word_cache_entry_t *adj_entry, *adj_tmp;
63   HASH_ITER(hh, g_adjectives_cache, adj_entry, adj_tmp) {
64     HASH_DEL(g_adjectives_cache, adj_entry);
65     SAFE_FREE(adj_entry->word);
66     SAFE_FREE(adj_entry);
67   }
68   g_adjectives_cache = NULL;
69 
70   // Cleanup nouns cache
71   word_cache_entry_t *noun_entry, *noun_tmp;
72   HASH_ITER(hh, g_nouns_cache, noun_entry, noun_tmp) {
73     HASH_DEL(g_nouns_cache, noun_entry);
74     SAFE_FREE(noun_entry->word);
75     SAFE_FREE(noun_entry);
76   }
77   g_nouns_cache = NULL;
78 
79   log_dev("Session string word cache cleaned up");
80 }






References lifecycle_shutdown(), and
    word_cache_entry_t::word.


Referenced by asciichat_shared_destroy().






bool is_session_string (const char * str)


Definition at line 254 of file
  discovery/strings.c.


254                                         {
255   if (!str || str[0] == '\0') {
256     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Session string is NULL or empty");
257     return false;
258   }
259 
260   size_t len = strlen(str);
261 
262   // Check length bounds (min 4, max defined by SESSION_STRING_MAX_LEN)
263   if (len < 4 || len > SESSION_STRING_MAX_LEN) {
264     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Session string length %zu outside valid range 4-%d", len, SESSION_STRING_MAX_LEN);
265     return false;
266   }
267 
268   // Session strings must be ASCII-only (homograph attack prevention)
269   if (!utf8_is_ascii_only(str)) {
270     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Session string contains non-ASCII characters");
271     return false;
272   }
273 
274   // Get compiled regex (lazy initialization)
275   pcre2_code *regex = session_format_regex_get();
276   if (!regex) {
277     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Session string PCRE2 regex failed to initialize");
278     return false;
279   }
280 
281   // Validate format using PCRE2 regex
282   pcre2_match_data *match_data = pcre2_match_data_create_from_pattern(regex, NULL);
283   if (!match_data) {
284     log_error("Failed to allocate match data for session string regex");
285     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate match data");
286     return false;
287   }
288 
289   int match_result = pcre2_jit_match(regex, (PCRE2_SPTR8)str, len, 0, 0, match_data, NULL);
290 
291   pcre2_match_data_free(match_data);
292 
293   if (match_result < 0) {
294     // Format validation failed
295     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Session string format does not match pattern");
296     return false;
297   }
298 
299   // Extract the three words by finding hyphens
300   // Format is guaranteed to be: word-word-word where each word is 2-12 lowercase letters
301   const char *hyphen1 = strchr(str, '-');
302   if (!hyphen1) {
303     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Session string missing first hyphen");
304     return false;
305   }
306 
307   const char *hyphen2 = strchr(hyphen1 + 1, '-');
308   if (!hyphen2) {
309     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Session string missing second hyphen");
310     return false;
311   }
312 
313   // Extract three words
314   size_t adj_len = hyphen1 - str;
315   size_t noun1_len = hyphen2 - hyphen1 - 1;
316   size_t noun2_len = len - (hyphen2 - str) - 1;
317 
318   if (adj_len >= 32 || noun1_len >= 32 || noun2_len >= 32) {
319     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Session string word length out of bounds");
320     return false;
321   }
322 
323   char adj[32], noun1[32], noun2[32];
324   memcpy(adj, str, adj_len);
325   adj[adj_len] = '\0';
326   memcpy(noun1, hyphen1 + 1, noun1_len);
327   noun1[noun1_len] = '\0';
328   memcpy(noun2, hyphen2 + 1, noun2_len);
329   noun2[noun2_len] = '\0';
330 
331   // Lazy initialization: build validation caches on first use
332   // call_once ensures thread-safe exactly-once execution
333   build_validation_caches();
334   // If initialization fails silently (logs warning), hashtables will be empty
335   // and validation will fail, which is safe behavior
336 
337   // Validate first word is an adjective
338   word_cache_entry_t *adj_entry = NULL;
339   HASH_FIND_STR(g_adjectives_cache, adj, adj_entry);
340   if (!adj_entry) {
341     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Session string first word '%s' is not a valid adjective", adj);
342     return false;
343   }
344 
345   // Validate second and third words are nouns
346   word_cache_entry_t *noun_entry1 = NULL;
347   HASH_FIND_STR(g_nouns_cache, noun1, noun_entry1);
348   if (!noun_entry1) {
349     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Session string second word '%s' is not a valid noun", noun1);
350     return false;
351   }
352 
353   word_cache_entry_t *noun_entry2 = NULL;
354   HASH_FIND_STR(g_nouns_cache, noun2, noun_entry2);
355   if (!noun_entry2) {
356     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Session string third word '%s' is not a valid noun", noun2);
357     return false;
358   }
359 
360   log_dev("Valid session string: %s", str);
361   return true;
362 }






References utf8_is_ascii_only().


Referenced by database_session_create(),
    discover_session_parallel(), and parse_client_address().
